 {"id":"bd-904","title":"Implement detect_collisions()","description":"**Warn about path collisions**

## What
Function to detect when different repos would resolve to the same local path.

## Why
With flat layout, org1/utils and org2/utils collide. Users need to know before we overwrite.

## Difference from dedupe
- dedupe: Same repo referenced twice (ok, skip second)
- collision: Different repos mapping to same path (warning!)

## Implementation
```bash
detect_collisions() {
    local -A path_to_url
    local collisions=0
    
    while IFS= read -r spec; do
        local url branch local_name
        parse_repo_spec "$spec" url branch local_name
        
        local path
        path=$(url_to_local_path "$url" "$PROJECTS_DIR" "$LAYOUT")
        
        local norm_url
        norm_url=$(normalize_url "$url")
        
        if [[ -n "${path_to_url[$path]:-}" ]]; then
            local existing="${path_to_url[$path]}"
            if [[ "$existing" != "$norm_url" ]]; then
                log_warn "Collision detected: $path"
                log_warn "  URL 1: $existing"
                log_warn "  URL 2: $norm_url"
                ((collisions++))
            fi
        else
            path_to_url[$path]="$norm_url"
        fi
    done
    
    return $collisions
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- Detects different repos at same path
- Doesn't flag same repo twice
- Returns collision count","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-03T16:10:05.881422408-05:00","updated_at":"2026-01-03T16:33:03.266022614-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-03T16:33:03.266022614-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented all repo list management functions: parse_repo_spec (namerefs), load_repo_list, dedupe_repos, detect_collisions, get_all_repos.  {"id":"bd-905","title":"Implement get_all_repos()","description":"**Load and merge all repository lists**

## What
Function to load all list files, merge, dedupe, and check for collisions.

## Why
This is the main entry point for getting the list of repos to process.

## Implementation
```bash
get_all_repos() {
    local repos_dir="$RU_CONFIG_DIR/repos.d"
    
    if [[ ! -d "$repos_dir" ]]; then
        log_warn "No repos.d directory. Run 'ru init' first."
        return 0
    fi
    
    # Collect all repos from all .txt files
    local all_repos
    all_repos=$(
        for f in "$repos_dir"/*.txt; do
            [[ -f "$f" ]] || continue
            load_repo_list "$f"
        done
    )
    
    if [[ -z "$all_repos" ]]; then
        log_warn "No repositories configured. Run 'ru add <repo>' first."
        return 0
    fi
    
    # Check for collisions (warning only)
    echo "$all_repos" | detect_collisions || true
    
    # Dedupe and output
    echo "$all_repos" | dedupe_repos
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- Loads from all .txt files in repos.d
- Warns about collisions
- Dedupes results
- Handles empty gracefully","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-03T16:10:05.881422408-05:00","updated_at":"2026-01-03T16:33:03.267327372-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-03T16:33:03.267327372-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented all repo list management functions: parse_repo_spec (namerefs), load_repo_list, dedupe_repos, detect_collisions, get_all_repos.  {"id":"bd-905b","title":"Define collision handling policy","description":"**Clear policy for path collision handling**

## What
Define what happens when collision is detected (different repos -> same path).

## Why
Current plan detects collisions but doesn't specify the behavior.

## Policy Options
1. **Warn and skip second repo** (safest, current implicit behavior)
2. **Fail fast** (strict, stops processing)
3. **Prompt user** (interactive only)
4. **Auto-rename with suffix** (risky)

## Chosen Policy: Warn and skip
```
Collision detected:
  Path: /home/user/projects/utils
  Configured: org1/utils (will be synced)
  Skipped:    org2/utils (same path)

To fix: Change layout to 'owner-repo' in config:
  ru config --set LAYOUT=owner-repo
```

## Implementation
- First occurrence wins
- Log warning with resolution hint
- Include in summary as 'skipped:collision'
- Exit code 2 (conflicts) if any collisions

## Acceptance Criteria
- Clear messaging about collision
- First-wins policy
- Resolution hint in output
- Documented in README","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-03T16:10:05.881422408-05:00","updated_at":"2026-01-03T16:30:11.250738889-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-03T16:30:11.250738889-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented in ru script SECTION 9B","labels":["lists","safety"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-905b","depends_on_id":"bd-9","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-03T16:14:13.858988726-05:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"bd-905b","depends_on_id":"bd-904","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-03T16:14:13.890572977-05:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"}]}

 {"id":"bd-9j92","title":"Implement main dashboard view (ntm mode)","description":"Task: Implement Main Dashboard View

Purpose
-------
Full-screen TUI dashboard showing all pending questions, active sessions,
and summary statistics in a organized layout.

Layout Design
-------------
+-----------------------------------------------------------------------+
|  ru review                                    Progress: 5/8  Runtime  |
+-----------------------------------------------------------------------+
|                                                                       |
|  PENDING QUESTIONS (3)                                                |
|  -------------------------------------------------------------------- |
|  [1] ● project-alpha     Issue #42     Priority: CRITICAL             |
|      Context: Authentication failing on Windows                       |
|      > a) Quick fix  b) Full refactor  c) Skip                       |
|                                                                       |
|  [2] ○ project-beta      PR #15        Priority: NORMAL              |
|      [Press Enter to expand]                                          |
|                                                                       |
+-----------------------------------------------------------------------+
|  ACTIVE SESSIONS                                                      |
|  -------------------------------------------------------------------- |
|  Repo              State         Progress      Health                 |
|  project-delta     GENERATING    80%           Healthy                |
|  project-epsilon   THINKING      60%           Healthy                |
+-----------------------------------------------------------------------+
|  SUMMARY                                                              |
|  Completed: 4 | Issues: 7 | PRs: 1 | Commits: 12                     |
+-----------------------------------------------------------------------+
| [1-9] Answer [Enter] Expand [d] Drill [s] Skip [a] Apply [q] Quit    |
+-----------------------------------------------------------------------+

Implementation
--------------

Dashboard State
  declare -A DASHBOARD_STATE=(
    [selected_index]=0
    [expanded_question]=""
    [scroll_offset]=0
    [panel_focus]="questions"
  )

render_dashboard()
  clear
  render_header
  render_questions_panel
  render_sessions_panel
  render_summary_panel
  render_footer

render_questions_panel()
  Load questions from queue
  For each question:
    if expanded: show full context
    else: show one-line summary
  Highlight selected with ANSI

render_sessions_panel()
  Query session states
  Format as table with columns

render_summary_panel()
  Load metrics from state
  Format as single line stats

Dashboard Loop
--------------
run_dashboard()
  while true:
    render_dashboard
    read -rsn1 key
    handle_keypress "$key"
    
    # Check for new events
    poll_events

handle_keypress()
  case key:
    1-9: select_and_answer
    Enter: toggle_expand
    d: open_drilldown
    s: skip_current
    q: confirm_quit

Refresh Logic
-------------
- Full refresh every 5 seconds
- Immediate refresh on user action
- Poll for new questions/events

Terminal Handling
-----------------
- Save cursor position
- Use alternate screen buffer
- Restore on exit
- Handle resize (SIGWINCH)

Testing
-------
- Verify layout renders correctly
- Verify keyboard navigation
- Verify refresh updates data
- Verify resize handling
- Test with different terminal sizes

Acceptance Criteria
-------------------
- [ ] Dashboard renders cleanly
- [ ] Questions panel shows all pending
- [ ] Sessions panel updates in real-time
- [ ] Keyboard shortcuts work
- [ ] Handles terminal resize","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-04T15:40:43.609323436-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-04T17:59:43.881389549-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-04T17:59:43.881389549-05:00","close_reason":"Dashboard view fully implemented with questions panel, sessions panel, summary panel, keyboard navigation, and terminal handling","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-9j92","depends_on_id":"bd-4ps0","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-04T16:08:34.753044804-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"bd-9s7y","title":"Implement local driver (tmux + stream-json)","description":"# Task: Implement Local Driver (tmux + stream-json)

## Purpose
Implement the session driver interface using tmux and Claude's stream-json output mode. This is the fallback driver when ntm is not available.

## Background
Claude Code supports --output-format stream-json which emits NDJSON events:
- System init with session_id and available tools
- Assistant messages with text and tool_use
- Tool results
- Final result with status

We capture this output and parse it for question detection.

## Implementation

### local_driver_start_session()
```bash
local_driver_start_session() {
    local wt_path="$1"
    local session_name="$2"
    local prompt="$3"
    
    local log_file="$wt_path/.ru/session.log"
    local event_pipe="$wt_path/.ru/events.pipe"
    
    # Create named pipe for event streaming
    rm -f "$event_pipe"
    mkfifo "$event_pipe"
    
    # Build claude command
    local claude_cmd="claude -p $(printf '%q' "$prompt") --output-format stream-json"
    
    # Create tmux session
    tmux new-session -d -s "$session_name" -c "$wt_path" \
        "exec $claude_cmd 2>&1 | tee '$log_file' > '$event_pipe'"
    
    # Return session info
    cat << EOF
{
  "session_id": "$session_name",
  "worktree": "$wt_path",
  "log_file": "$log_file",
  "event_pipe": "$event_pipe",
  "started_at": "$(date -u +%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ)"
}
EOF
}
```

### local_driver_send_to_session()
```bash
local_driver_send_to_session() {
    local session_id="$1"
    local message="$2"
    
    # Send via tmux
    tmux send-keys -t "$session_id" "$message" Enter
    
    return $?
}
```

### local_driver_get_session_state()
```bash
local_driver_get_session_state() {
    local session_id="$1"
    
    # Check if tmux session exists
    if ! tmux has-session -t "$session_id" 2>/dev/null; then
        echo '{"state": "complete", "reason": "session_ended"}'
        return
    fi
    
    # Capture last lines of output
    local output
    output=$(tmux capture-pane -t "$session_id" -p -S -50 2>/dev/null)
    
    # Detect state from output
    local state="generating"
    local wait_reason=""
    
    # Check for prompt (waiting)
    if echo "$output" | grep -qE '^\s*>\s*$|Press Enter|Select.*:|Enter.*:'; then
        state="waiting"
        wait_reason="prompt_detected"
    fi
    
    # Check for errors
    if echo "$output" | grep -qiE 'error:|panic:|rate.limit|429'; then
        state="error"
    fi
    
    cat << EOF
{
  "state": "$state",
  "wait_reason": "$wait_reason",
  "session_id": "$session_id"
}
EOF
}
```

### local_driver_stream_events()
```bash
local_driver_stream_events() {
    local session_id="$1"
    local callback="$2"
    local wt_path="$3"
    
    local event_pipe="$wt_path/.ru/events.pipe"
    local log_file="$wt_path/.ru/session.log"
    
    # Parse stream-json events
    while IFS= read -r line; do
        [[ -z "$line" ]] && continue
        
        # Parse JSON event type
        local event_type
        event_type=$(echo "$line" | jq -r '.type // empty' 2>/dev/null)
        
        case "$event_type" in
            system)
                local session_id_from_event
                session_id_from_event=$(echo "$line" | jq -r '.session_id // empty')
                $callback "init" "$session_id_from_event"
                ;;
            assistant)
                # Check for AskUserQuestion tool_use
                local tool_name
                tool_name=$(echo "$line" | jq -r \
                    '.message.content[]? | select(.type=="tool_use") | .name // empty' 2>/dev/null)
                
                if [[ "$tool_name" == "AskUserQuestion" ]]; then
                    local question_data
                    question_data=$(echo "$line" | jq -c \
                        '.message.content[] | select(.name=="AskUserQuestion") | .input')
                    $callback "question" "$question_data"
                fi
                ;;
            result)
                local status
                status=$(echo "$line" | jq -r '.status // "unknown"')
                $callback "complete" "$status"
                break
                ;;
        esac
    done < "$event_pipe"
}
```

### local_driver_stop_session()
```bash
local_driver_stop_session() {
    local session_id="$1"
    
    # Kill tmux session
    tmux kill-session -t "$session_id" 2>/dev/null || true
    
    return 0
}
```

### local_driver_interrupt_session()
```bash
local_driver_interrupt_session() {
    local session_id="$1"
    
    # Send Ctrl+C
    tmux send-keys -t "$session_id" C-c
    
    return 0
}
```

## Session Monitoring Loop
```bash
local_driver_monitor_sessions() {
    local -a sessions=("$@")
    
    while [[ ${#sessions[@]} -gt 0 ]]; do
        local -a active_sessions=()
        
        for session_id in "${sessions[@]}"; do
            local state
            state=$(local_driver_get_session_state "$session_id" | jq -r '.state')
            
            case "$state" in
                complete)
                    handle_session_complete "$session_id"
                    ;;
                error)
                    handle_session_error "$session_id"
                    ;;
                waiting)
                    handle_session_waiting "$session_id"
                    active_sessions+=("$session_id")
                    ;;
                *)
                    active_sessions+=("$session_id")
                    ;;
            esac
        done
        
        sessions=("${active_sessions[@]}")
        sleep 2
    done
}
```

## Testing
- Launch session, verify tmux created
- Send message, verify delivered
- Detect AskUserQuestion event
- Detect session completion
- Interrupt and stop work correctly

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Sessions launch in tmux with stream-json
- [ ] Events parsed from NDJSON stream
- [ ] AskUserQuestion detected
- [ ] Messages sent via tmux send-keys
- [ ] Session cleanup works
- [ ] Works without ntm installed","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-04T15:35:14.53945729-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-04T16:42:46.375298473-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-04T16:42:46.375298473-05:00","close_reason":"Local driver implemented with: local_driver_start_session (tmux + stream-json), local_driver_send_to_session, local_driver_get_session_state, local_driver_stop_session, local_driver_interrupt_session, local_driver_stream_events (stub for bd-8zt6), local_driver_list_sessions, local_driver_session_alive. Driver routing via _enable_local_driver(). ShellCheck passes.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-9s7y","depends_on_id":"bd-jm89","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-04T15:44:54.79309431-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"bd-bxii","title":"Phase 4: TUI (Dashboard, Drill-down, Keyboard Shortcuts)","description":"Phase 4: TUI (Terminal User Interface)

Overview
--------
Build the human interface for reviewing aggregated questions, monitoring
session progress, and making decisions across all repos.

Why TUI?
--------
- Centralized view of all pending questions
- Quick keyboard-driven decisions
- Session monitoring at a glance
- Drill-down for context when needed
- Works over SSH, in tmux, etc.

Components
----------

4.1 Basic Mode (gum-based)
   Fallback when advanced TUI not available:
   - gum style for boxes and formatting
   - gum choose for option selection
   - gum confirm for yes/no
   - Sequential question presentation

4.2 Main Dashboard
   Full-screen TUI with panels:
   - PENDING QUESTIONS: Priority-sorted list
   - ACTIVE SESSIONS: State and progress
   - SUMMARY: Stats and metrics
   - Footer: Keyboard shortcuts

4.3 Drill-down View
   Detailed view for single question:
   - Full context from agent
   - Issue/PR details
   - Patch summary (files changed)
   - Answer options with descriptions
   - Back button to dashboard

4.4 Keyboard Shortcuts
   Efficient keyboard-driven UX:
   - 1-9: Quick answer selection
   - Enter: Expand/collapse
   - d: Drill-down
   - s: Skip current
   - S: Skip all (with confirm)
   - z: Snooze (1d/7d/30d)
   - t: Insert template
   - b: Bulk apply safe changes
   - a: Apply approved changes
   - p/r: Pause/resume
   - q: Quit

4.5 Snooze Feature
   Defer items without skipping permanently:
   - 1 day / 7 days / 30 days
   - Stored in snoozed.json
   - Checked during discovery

4.6 Response Templates
   Pre-configured responses:
   - stale-issue.md
   - duplicate.md
   - needs-info.md
   - wontfix.md
   - thank-you.md

4.7 Bulk Apply
   Apply all low-risk changes that pass gates:
   - Scan plans for risk_level=low
   - Verify tests passed
   - Verify no unanswered questions
   - Confirm before applying

4.8 Patch Summary View
   Show what changed before approving:
   - Branch name
   - Commit count
   - Files changed with diff stats
   - Test status (PASS/FAIL)
   - gh_actions pending

Exit Criteria
-------------
- Dashboard shows all pending questions
- Drill-down provides full context
- Keyboard shortcuts work reliably
- Snooze persists across sessions
- Templates load and apply
- Bulk apply works for safe changes

Estimated Effort
----------------

 {"id":"bd-cuq5","title":"Phase 5: Apply Phase (Quality Gates, gh_actions, Push)","description":"Phase 5: Apply Phase

Overview
--------
Execute approved actions from review plans: run quality gates, perform
GitHub mutations (comments, closes, labels), and push changes.

Why Separate Phase?
-------------------
- Plan mode is safe (no mutations)
- User reviews plans before execution
- Quality gates catch issues before push
- Explicit --apply flag required
- Auditability of what was done

Components
----------

5.1 Quality Gates Framework
   Run tests/lint before allowing push:
   - Per-repo policy configuration
   - Auto-detect test commands
   - Fail blocks push
   - Secret scanning

5.2 Per-Repo Policy Configuration
   ~/.config/ru/review-policies.d/owner_repo.conf:
   - REVIEW_TEST_CMD
   - REVIEW_LINT_CMD
   - REVIEW_REQUIRE_TESTS
   - Custom validation scripts

5.3 gh_actions Execution
   Execute actions from review-plan.json:
   - comment: gh issue comment / gh pr comment
   - close: gh issue close / gh pr close
   - label: gh issue edit --add-label
   - (merge not supported - policy says no PRs)

5.4 Worktree Merge and Push
   Merge worktree changes to main:
   - Verify quality gates passed
   - Merge worktree branch to base
   - Push to remote
   - Clean up worktree

5.5 Apply Progress Tracking
   Track what has been applied:
   - Mark items as applied in state
   - Log all gh_actions executed
   - Record push timestamps
   - Enable partial retry

5.6 Rollback Capability
   If something goes wrong:
   - Worktrees preserved until explicit cleanup
   - Can revert commits locally
   - gh_actions not reversible (comments stay)
   - Plan artifact serves as audit log

Exit Criteria
-------------
- Quality gates run before push
- gh_actions execute correctly
- Push only with explicit --push flag
- State tracks what was applied
- Rollback path documented

Estimated Effort
----------------

-{"id":"bd-cutq","title":"Implement per-repo review policy configuration","description":"# Task: Implement Per-Repo Review Policy Configuration

## Purpose
Allow users to customize review behavior per repository through configuration files. Different repos have different test commands, lint requirements, and review policies.

## Background
Not all repos are the same:
- Some have Makefile, others use npm
- Some require strict lint, others are more lenient
- Some repos should never auto-push
- Some repos need specific branch protection rules

## Configuration Structure

### Directory Layout
```
~/.config/ru/review-policies.d/
├── _default.conf          # Default policy (applies to all)
├── owner_repo1.conf       # Policy for owner/repo1
├── owner_repo2.conf       # Policy for owner/repo2
└── team_*.conf            # Glob patterns work too
```

### Policy File Format
```bash
# ~/.config/ru/review-policies.d/myorg_backend.conf

# Test configuration
REVIEW_TEST_CMD="make test"
REVIEW_TEST_TIMEOUT=300          # seconds

# Lint configuration
REVIEW_LINT_CMD="npm run lint"
REVIEW_LINT_REQUIRED=true        # Block push if lint fails

# Secret scanning
REVIEW_SECRET_SCAN=true
REVIEW_SECRET_PATTERNS="AWS_|PRIVATE_KEY|password"

# Push policy
REVIEW_ALLOW_PUSH=true           # false = never push for this repo
REVIEW_REQUIRE_APPROVAL=false    # true = always ask before push

# Priority overrides
REVIEW_BASE_PRIORITY=50          # Add to all items from this repo
REVIEW_LABELS_BOOST="urgent:30,security:40"

# Review behavior
REVIEW_MAX_ITEMS=10              # Max items to review per session
REVIEW_SKIP_PRS=false            # true = only review issues
REVIEW_DEEP_MODE=false           # true = comprehensive review
```

### Default Policy
```bash
# ~/.config/ru/review-policies.d/_default.conf

REVIEW_TEST_CMD=""               # Auto-detect
REVIEW_LINT_CMD=""
REVIEW_LINT_REQUIRED=false
REVIEW_SECRET_SCAN=true
REVIEW_ALLOW_PUSH=true
REVIEW_REQUIRE_APPROVAL=false
REVIEW_BASE_PRIORITY=0
REVIEW_MAX_ITEMS=20
REVIEW_SKIP_PRS=false
REVIEW_DEEP_MODE=false
```

## Implementation

### load_repo_policy()
```bash
load_repo_policy() {
    local repo_id="$1"
    local policy_dir="$RU_CONFIG_DIR/review-policies.d"
    
    # Reset to defaults first
    source_default_policy
    
    # Try exact match
    local exact_file="$policy_dir/${repo_id//\//_}.conf"
    if [[ -f "$exact_file" ]]; then
        source "$exact_file"
        log_verbose "Loaded policy: $exact_file"
        return 0
    fi
    
    # Try glob patterns (owner_*.conf, team_*.conf)
    local owner="${repo_id%%/*}"
    for pattern_file in "$policy_dir"/"${owner}"_*.conf "$policy_dir"/*_*.conf; do
        if [[ -f "$pattern_file" ]]; then
            # Check if pattern matches
            local pattern_base
            pattern_base=$(basename "$pattern_file" .conf)
            if [[ "$repo_id" == ${pattern_base//_/\/} || \
                  "$repo_id" == ${pattern_base//_/}* ]]; then
                source "$pattern_file"
                log_verbose "Loaded policy via pattern: $pattern_file"
                return 0
            fi
        fi
    done
    
    log_verbose "Using default policy for $repo_id"
    return 0
}
```

### source_default_policy()
```bash
source_default_policy() {
    local default_file="$RU_CONFIG_DIR/review-policies.d/_default.conf"
    
    # Built-in defaults
    REVIEW_TEST_CMD=""
    REVIEW_TEST_TIMEOUT=300
    REVIEW_LINT_CMD=""
    REVIEW_LINT_REQUIRED=false
    REVIEW_SECRET_SCAN=true
    REVIEW_ALLOW_PUSH=true
    REVIEW_REQUIRE_APPROVAL=false
    REVIEW_BASE_PRIORITY=0
    REVIEW_LABELS_BOOST=""
    REVIEW_MAX_ITEMS=20
    REVIEW_SKIP_PRS=false
    REVIEW_DEEP_MODE=false
    
    # Override with user defaults if present
    [[ -f "$default_file" ]] && source "$default_file"
}
```

### apply_policy_priority_boost()
```bash
apply_policy_priority_boost() {
    local repo_id="$1"
    local base_score="$2"
    local labels="$3"
    
    load_repo_policy "$repo_id"
    
    local score=$base_score
    
    # Add base priority boost
    score=$((score + REVIEW_BASE_PRIORITY))
    
    # Apply label boosts
    if [[ -n "$REVIEW_LABELS_BOOST" ]]; then
        IFS="," read -ra boosts <<< "$REVIEW_LABELS_BOOST"
        for boost in "${boosts[@]}"; do
            local label="${boost%%:*}"
            local points="${boost##*:}"
            if echo "$labels" | grep -qi "$label"; then
                score=$((score + points))
            fi
        done
    fi
    
    echo "$score"
}
```

### validate_policy_file()
```bash
validate_policy_file() {
    local file="$1"
    
    # Check syntax
    if ! bash -n "$file" 2>/dev/null; then
        log_error "Syntax error in policy file: $file"
        return 1
    fi
    
    # Check for required variables
    source "$file"
    
    # Validate values
    if [[ -n "$REVIEW_TEST_TIMEOUT" ]] && ! [[ "$REVIEW_TEST_TIMEOUT" =~ ^[0-9]+$ ]]; then
        log_error "Invalid REVIEW_TEST_TIMEOUT in $file"
        return 1
    fi
    
    return 0
}
```

### init_review_policies()
```bash
init_review_policies() {
    local policy_dir="$RU_CONFIG_DIR/review-policies.d"
    
    if [[ ! -d "$policy_dir" ]]; then
        mkdir -p "$policy_dir"
        
        # Create example default policy
        cat > "$policy_dir/_default.conf.example" << "EOF"
# Default review policy (rename to _default.conf to activate)
# These settings apply to all repos unless overridden

REVIEW_TEST_CMD=""               # Auto-detect (Makefile, package.json, etc.)
REVIEW_LINT_CMD=""               # Optional lint command
REVIEW_SECRET_SCAN=true          # Scan for secrets before push
REVIEW_ALLOW_PUSH=true           # Allow pushing changes
REVIEW_REQUIRE_APPROVAL=false    # Require confirmation before push
EOF
        log_info "Created example policy file: $policy_dir/_default.conf.example"
    fi
}
```

## Integration Points
- Called by priority scoring (apply_policy_priority_boost)
- Called by quality gates (load test/lint commands)
- Called by push phase (check REVIEW_ALLOW_PUSH)
- Called during cmd_review init

## Testing
- Verify default policy applied
- Verify exact match policy overrides default
- Verify glob pattern matching works
- Verify invalid policy files rejected
- Verify priority boosts applied correctly

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Default policy applied to all repos
- [ ] Per-repo policies override defaults
- [ ] Glob patterns work for org-wide settings
- [ ] Policy validation catches errors
- [ ] Example policy created on init

+{"id":"bd-cutq","title":"Implement per-repo review policy configuration","description":"# Task: Implement Per-Repo Review Policy Configuration

## Purpose
Allow users to customize review behavior per repository through configuration files. Different repos have different test commands, lint requirements, and review policies.

## Background
Not all repos are the same:
- Some have Makefile, others use npm
- Some require strict lint, others are more lenient
- Some repos should never auto-push
- Some repos need specific branch protection rules

## Configuration Structure

### Directory Layout
```
~/.config/ru/review-policies.d/
├── _default.conf          # Default policy (applies to all)
├── owner_repo1.conf       # Policy for owner/repo1
├── owner_repo2.conf       # Policy for owner/repo2
└── team_*.conf            # Glob patterns work too
```

### Policy File Format
```bash
# ~/.config/ru/review-policies.d/myorg_backend.conf

# Test configuration
REVIEW_TEST_CMD="make test"
REVIEW_TEST_TIMEOUT=300          # seconds

# Lint configuration
REVIEW_LINT_CMD="npm run lint"
REVIEW_LINT_REQUIRED=true        # Block push if lint fails

# Secret scanning
REVIEW_SECRET_SCAN=true
REVIEW_SECRET_PATTERNS="AWS_|PRIVATE_KEY|password"

# Push policy
REVIEW_ALLOW_PUSH=true           # false = never push for this repo
REVIEW_REQUIRE_APPROVAL=false    # true = always ask before push

# Priority overrides
REVIEW_BASE_PRIORITY=50          # Add to all items from this repo
REVIEW_LABELS_BOOST="urgent:30,security:40"

# Review behavior
REVIEW_MAX_ITEMS=10              # Max items to review per session
REVIEW_SKIP_PRS=false            # true = only review issues
REVIEW_DEEP_MODE=false           # true = comprehensive review
```

### Default Policy
```bash
# ~/.config/ru/review-policies.d/_default.conf

REVIEW_TEST_CMD=""               # Auto-detect
REVIEW_LINT_CMD=""
REVIEW_LINT_REQUIRED=false
REVIEW_SECRET_SCAN=true
REVIEW_ALLOW_PUSH=true
REVIEW_REQUIRE_APPROVAL=false
REVIEW_BASE_PRIORITY=0
REVIEW_MAX_ITEMS=20
REVIEW_SKIP_PRS=false
REVIEW_DEEP_MODE=false
```

### load_repo_policy()
```bash
load_repo_policy() {
    local repo_id="$1"
    local policy_dir="$RU_CONFIG_DIR/review-policies.d"
    
    # Reset to defaults first
    source_default_policy
    
    # Try exact match

    if [[ -f "$exact_file" ]]; then

    fi
    
    # Try glob patterns (owner_*.conf, team_*.conf)
    local owner="${repo_id%%/*}"
    for pattern_file in "$policy_dir"/"${owner}"_*.conf "$policy_dir"/*_*.conf; do
        if [[ -f "$pattern_file" ]]; then
            # Check if pattern matches

                  "$repo_id" == ${pattern_base//_/}* ]]; then

                log_verbose "Loaded policy via pattern: $pattern_file"
                return 0
            fi
        fi
    done

    return 0
}
```
